title: Real Options

content.statement: Keeping your options open is not indecision; under uncertainty it is an asset you can price.
content.explainer: A real option is the right, but not the obligation, to make a business move later — expand, delay, abandon, switch. When the future is uncertain, holding that right has value because you can act if things go well and walk away if they don't; that value rises with uncertainty and falls with the cost of keeping the option open.
questions: Are you committing fully today when a small payment to keep the choice open would let you decide with better information later?
What is the upside you would capture if you preserved the right to act, and how likely is it actually to materialize?
Which 'options' are you paying to keep open that have near-zero probability of ever paying off, and should just be closed?
sop: Name the decision you could defer and the future move it would preserve (expand, delay, abandon, or switch).
Estimate the Upside in dollars if you keep the option open and later exercise it well.
Estimate the Probability that the favorable scenario actually occurs (see calculation, page 6).
Estimate the Cost to Hold Open — the fee, retainer, or carrying cost of preserving the right without committing.
Compute Option Value and keep the option only when it is clearly positive; otherwise commit or walk away.

definitions.def: The right, but not the obligation, to take a future business action — such as expanding, delaying, or abandoning — whose value comes from flexibility under uncertainty.
What you pay to keep a choice available without committing — a deposit, retainer, lease, or the carrying cost of not deciding yet.

content.equation: Option Value = (Upside × Probability) − Cost to Hold Open
content.equation_example: (Upside $100,000 × Probability 0.3) − Cost to Hold Open $10,000 = $30,000 − $10,000 = $20,000. A positive value means paying to keep the choice open is worth it; if the cost to hold open exceeded $30,000, the option would be negative and you should commit or drop it now.
content.closing_statement: Flexibility is worth paying for exactly when you cannot yet see which way the world will break.
content.question: Are you committing fully today when a small payment to keep the choice open would let you decide with better information later?

caption:
Principle Nº 353: Real Options

Keeping your options open is not indecision; under uncertainty it is an asset you can price.

Ask yourself: Are you committing fully today when a small payment to keep the choice open would let you decide with better information later?
